before_model.pattern: Service class — ProfileService with direct repository calls
before_model.profile_creation: InsertProfile(Profile) — caller responsible for mapping + validation
before_model.profile_deletion: no explicit delete — profiles marked inactive or removed via bulk ops
before_model.profile_listing: GetProfilesbyUserId with filter params — complex signature, 5 params
before_model.authorization: CanUserAccessProfile check scattered at call sites
before_model.tenant_isolation: customerId passed as parameter to service methods

after_model.pattern: Vertical slice — Handler + Endpoint + SQL per behavior (4 behaviors)
after_model.profile_creation: CreateProfile handler — validates name uniqueness, inserts, returns ProfileId
after_model.profile_deletion: DeleteProfile handler — soft-delete via DeletedAt, explicit intent
after_model.profile_listing: ListProfilesForUser handler — scoped to UserId + CustomerId from ICurrentUser
after_model.authorization: ICurrentUser provides UserId + CustomerId — no scattered access checks
after_model.tenant_isolation: CustomerId from ICurrentUser — not passed as param

design_decisions.decision: Split 5-param GetProfilesbyUserId into dedicated ListProfilesForUser handler
design_decisions.reason: sms-service had overloaded filter signature — green-ai CQRS means one handler, one intent

design_decisions.decision: Add explicit DeleteProfile as first-class behavior
design_decisions.reason: sms-service had no explicit delete path — green-ai makes lifecycle explicit

simplifications.reason: green-ai profile model does not have system-profile concept — scope reduced to user-owned profiles

transformations: UpdateProfile → UpdateProfile handler (explicit command, single SQL)
no equivalent → DeleteProfile handler (new explicit behavior)
removed_concepts: includeSystemProfile filter param (no concept in green-ai)
5-param overloaded service signature (split to CQRS handlers)
verdict: REDESIGNED — explicit lifecycle, CQRS split, ICurrentUser tenant isolation. Explainable without sms-service reference.
